blurb: macOS app security extends the App Sandbox and Hardened Runtime frameworks to protect against malware and privilege escalation. Apps with the App Sandbox entitlement are restricted to a defined container and must declare entitlements to access any external resources. The Hardened Runtime prevents code injection, dynamic library hijacking, and debugging by untrusted tools. Gatekeeper checks notarization status and developer code signatures at first launch, quarantining non-compliant apps. The TCC framework requires user consent for access to sensitive data categories including Documents, Desktop, Contacts, Calendar, Photos, and Microphone.
